articles.title: ‘It's an honor’: Trump interacts with AI-powered Theodore Roosevelt avatar during library visit; watch
articles.description: A viral video shows President Trump interacting with an AI-powered Theodore Roosevelt avatar at the Roosevelt Library in North Dakota.
articles.content: Trump first toured the library and then gave a speech outside the 96,000-square-foot facility built with a budget of $450 million. Trump honored the 26th US President by comparing Roosevelt to himself. Trump claimed, like him, Roosevelt had a "freaki... [1163 chars]

articles.title: CNA Explains: Why AI stocks are whipsawing markets from Wall Street to Seoul
articles.description: Are recent tech sell-offs a cause for concern, and is an AI bubble forming?
articles.content:
SINGAPORE: Tech stocks have been swinging sharply in recent weeks, as investors weigh the promise of artificial intelligence against concerns over lofty valuations and returns on massive spending.

articles.title: Commentary: The true cost of AI scams isn’t financial - it's human
articles.description: Governments and international organisations need to be more actively involved in how AI technology develops and to what purpose, says veteran newspaper editor Han Fook Kwang.
articles.content:
SINGAPORE: I was recently the subject of a piece of fake news in a scam to entice unsuspecting readers to part with their money.
The scammers published a completely fabricated story of me interviewing former transport minister S Iswaran after his rel... [6400 chars]

articles.title: Commentary: World Cup propels surveillance to new heights
articles.description: AI-driven surveillance is playing a major role across the World Cup, says this academic.
articles.content: RICHMOND, Virginia: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is the largest sporting event in history. It’s also the most surveilled World Cup ever. If you’re visiting or travelling around host cities, then you and your face, behaviour, movement and devices are being... [5948 chars]

articles.title: TESLA and SPACEX Can Get100 GIGAWATTS of Power for AI Data Centers Using Modular Power and AI
articles.description: Duke "Rethinking Load Growth" reports that having a flexible energy demand during 3 peak hours unlocks 20% more overall power. Curtailing just ~0.5–1% of
articles.content:
Duke “Rethinking Load Growth” reports that having a flexible energy demand during 3 peak hours unlocks 20% more overall power. Curtailing just ~0.5–1% of annual energy could unlock on the order of 76–100 GW of new load on today’s US grid.

articles.title: AI Spending Is Boosting Bank Stocks, Not Hurting Bond Markets
articles.description: AI borrowing is surging, but investors say it's not disrupting the bond market. Instead, banks are benefiting from the financing boom.
articles.content:
Wall Street is finding out just how deep its pockets really are.

articles.title: In A First, AI Decodes Entire Scroll Scorched In Mt. Vesuvius Eruption
articles.description: A full Herculaneum scroll "unwrapped" with technology reveals new texts, titles and authors unknown to history and ushers in a new era for the study of the ancient world.
articles.content: Using high-resolution X-ray imaging and artificial intelligence, scientists have for the first time virtually unrolled the surviving portion of an entire scroll scorched in the Mount Vesuvius eruption of 79 CE, viewing its contents from beginning to ... [3443 chars]

articles.title: Samsung Chases TSMC With Quantum-Powered Chipmaking as AI Reshapes the Most Critical Step in Fabrication
articles.description: Korean chip manufacturing giant Samsung is developing simulation technologies for lithography that rely on quantum computing and artificial intelligence, suggests a report from the Korean press. The technology will be used to run simulations of the first stage of the chip manufacturing process, and Samsung will also rely on artificial intelligence to streamline the process. Through the technology, the firm aims to reduce the time and cost of the lithography and etching processes. Samsung Is Developing Algorithms To Boost Its Photolithography, Say Sources In the semiconductor manufacturing process, lithography is the first and most important step. It involves high-end machines, […]

articles.title: Trump honors 'freakin' wild life' of Teddy Roosevelt, interacts with AI-powered ex-prez in North Dakota visit
articles.description: MEDORA, ND — President Trump paid tribute to Theodore Roosevelt as a “great man" while cutting the ribbon to his new presidential library in the Dakota Badlands — after interacting with an artificial intelligence-powered hologram of his predecessor.
